headlines.title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton to plead guilty to retaining national security information
headlines.description: John Bolton, who served as national security adviser during President Donald Trump’s first term and later became one of his fiercest critics, has agreed to plead guilty to one count of retaining national security information, two sources familiar with the matter said

headlines.title: Trump makes announcement on coal investments
headlines.description: Watch live coverage as President Trump makes an announcement on investments in coal projects.

headlines.title: Immigration lawyers slam scammers stealing their identities and offering fraudulent services
headlines.description: Immigration attorneys are having to take down sites and social media channels as scammers use AI, deepfakes and cloned domains to impersonate them and scam clients.

headlines.title: Marjane Satrapi, French-Iranian author of ‘Persepolis,’ dies at 56
headlines.description: Marjane Satrapi, the French-Iranian author and illustrator behind the popular graphic novel series and film “Persopolis,” has died, the French presidency said Thursday.

headlines.title: Woman finds moving worms and parasites in SpaghettiOs, lawsuit says
headlines.description: A mother in Florida is suing the Campbell’s Co.

headlines.title: Science reveals people are capable of multitasking — it just requires practice
headlines.description: New research reveals a mental workaround that is activated through repetition and experience.

headlines.title: Jozy Altidore, now a broadcaster, is bullish on the U.S. making a deep World Cup run
headlines.description: The former striker will serve as a World Cup commentator for Telemundo, the tournament’s official Spanish-language network.

headlines.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
headlines.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

headlines.title: Kennedy Center lawyers tell staff to remove references to Trump in signage
headlines.description: Attorneys for the iconic performing arts venue said the signage must revert to previous names like The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts by June 12.

headlines.title: From The Sports Desk: Jalen Brunson, ‘Captain Clutch,’ ices Game 1 for Knicks
headlines.description: The San Antonio Spurs had taken a 1-point lead, with just more than two minutes to play in the fourth quarter of Game 1 of the NBA Finals last night, when something remarkable happened.

headlines.title: Senate begins voting on Republican bill to fund ICE as GOP is split on Trump’s $1.8B fund
headlines.description: The Senate began voting on a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol as Republicans split on Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund.

headlines.title: Rare type of Lyme disease found for the first time in New York
headlines.description: A rare type of Lyme disease caused by B. mayonii bacteria was found for the first time in New York state. It was previously only ever detected in Minnesota and Wisconsin.
